title: Trichopilia pusilla
taxonomy: Trichopilia pusilla belongs to the family Orchidaceae, commonly known as the orchid family. This classification places Trichopilia pusilla within the larger group of flowering plants known as angiosperms. As a member of the Orchidaceae family, Trichopilia pusilla is further classified within the subfamily Cymbidioideae. The species is also classified within the tribe Cymbidieae. Within the tribe, Trichopilia pusilla is part of the subtribe Cymbidiinae.

intro: Trichopilia pusilla, also known as the "orchid tree," is a species of flowering plant in the orchid family, characterized by its delicate white flowers and slender leaves. Trichopilia pusilla has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, inflammation, and respiratory issues, due to its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and expectorant properties.
